scope: Semantic public API contracts that are exposed through strings, tags, generated routes, event topics, DTO field semantics, or runtime registration rather than only exported Go symbols.

package_reviews.summary: Application boot/module/DI helpers reviewed as the top-level framework entrypoint. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.
AI provider, agent, tool, message, and option APIs. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.
Streaming chunk/writer/builder APIs. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

Resource, operation, request, auth, router, and handler-extension APIs. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

Approval domain models, enums, node definitions, events, hooks, and extension interfaces. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.
Admin response DTOs for the built-in approval resource. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

Current-user approval response DTOs for the built-in approval resource. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

Cache interfaces, memory/Redis constructors, serializers, eviction, invalidation, and singleflight helpers. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

CLI implementation exports are not treated as import APIs, but the root command name, registered subcommands, and version output are user-facing command contracts. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger still cover exported implementation symbols, fields, and methods.

The generate-build-info command exposes stable flags, defaults, Git fallbacks, generated directory behavior, and generated Go source shape. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger still cover exported implementation symbols, fields, and methods.

The create command exposes command name, planned flags, required name behavior, and current not-implemented error as user-facing command contracts. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger still cover exported implementation symbols, fields, and methods.

The generate-model-schema command exposes stable flags, file/directory modes, model-selection rules, bun/label tag grammar, embedded-field traversal, and generated schema helper shape. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger still cover exported implementation symbols, fields, and methods.

All config structs, config keys, Effective methods, validation, and runtime defaults. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.
Request context key/get/set helpers. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

Copy options, built-in converter families, default case sensitivity, custom converter append behavior, field-name mapping, and merge behavior. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.
CQRS action, bus, handler, and command/query APIs. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.
Scheduler, job descriptor, options, and monitor APIs. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

CRUD builders, options, hooks, processors, import/export, tree, and query-extension APIs. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

Symmetric/asymmetric cipher interfaces, constructors, options, and errors. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.
CSV import/export helpers and options. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

Dynamic datasource provider, registry, reconcile, and registration option APIs. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

Database-adjacent duplicate/foreign-key helper detection codes, fallback message matching, and column alias formatting. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

Decimal wrapper, forwarded arithmetic/marshal methods, universal conversion input families, nil pointer handling, and unsupported-type error text. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

Event bus, typed publish/subscribe, route inspector, metrics, and publish/subscribe options. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.
Inbox repository and idempotency record APIs. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

Publish/consume middleware, tracing, logging, metrics, recover, and inbox middleware APIs. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

Transport, delivery, transactional transport, and subscription contracts. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.
In-process transport constructors and options. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

Persistent outbox transport, relay/repository contracts, rows, and options. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.
Redis Stream transport constructors and options. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.
Excel import/export helpers and options. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.
Expression engine API, options, values, and HTTP error codes. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

Hash and HMAC helpers expose stable algorithm choices, string/byte input families, and hex-encoded output lengths. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.
Fiber content-type and IP helpers. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.
Translator, dictionary, setup, and error APIs. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

Built-in embedded locale catalog entrypoint and shipped JSON language files. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

UUID v7, XID, random, and Snowflake ID generators expose stable output formats, default singletons, random alphabet/length defaults, Snowflake epoch/node/step configuration, and VEF_NODE_ID behavior. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

JS VM engine, require/module registry, and value conversion APIs. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

Logging interface, logger-configurable interface, level constants, Level.String output values, and root-package NamedLogger integration. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.
Map decoder and decode-hook APIs. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

MCP provider, definition, schema, and server-info extension APIs. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

SPA middleware configuration supplied through top-level helpers. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.
Mold transformer/translator/dictionary resolver APIs. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

Inclusive ordered range helper, validity/emptiness rules, overlap checks, and intersection behavior. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

Monitor service API, system DTOs, resource actions, and error codes. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

ORM model, DB/Tx, query/DDL/expression/condition builders, hooks, aliases, and enum families. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.
Pagination request and response helper. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

Password cipher/hash implementations, composite encoder, options, and errors. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

Pointer helper functions expose stable zero/nil, fallback, lazy fallback, equality, and coalescing behavior. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

Reflection, conversion, visitor, string-field, value, method-discovery, and cast helper APIs. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

Result envelope, errors, constructors/options, and status constants. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.
Schema service, DTOs, resource actions, and error codes. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

Search tag parser, range parser, applier, operator constants, and errors. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

Authentication, JWT, signature, challenge, RBAC, data-scope, principal, event, and error APIs. Security contracts are split across auth/wire, authorization/events, and data-permission runtime entries; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.
Sequence policy/rule/store/engine APIs. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.
Sort direction/nulls/order-spec primitives. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

Storage service/multipart/API resource, file refs, claim/delete lifecycle, ACL, URL mapper, events, and errors. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.
Struct tag and compact key/value parser APIs. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

Tabular schema, adapter, parser, formatter, resolver, and I/O APIs. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

Date/Time/DateTime parsing, formatting, JSON/text marshal APIs. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

Tree builder helpers expose adapter callback semantics, root/orphan handling, empty-ID behavior, cycle protection, and node/path search behavior. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

Validation entrypoints, custom rule/type registration, and built-in rules. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.
Framework version constant value. Semantic contracts are linked by contract_ids; generated public API index and symbol ledger cover exported symbols, fields, and methods.

entries.contract: AI SDK UI Message Stream SSE wire contract
entries.summary: The stream package writes AI SDK UI Message Stream-compatible SSE frames, including data-{type} chunks, lifecycle chunks, SSE headers, and the terminal data: [DONE] frame.

entries.contract: AI stream builder defaults and terminal paths
entries.summary: The stream builder exposes defaults, source handling, ID generation, EOF/error terminal behavior, callbacks, and JSON fallback behavior as user-visible runtime contracts.

entries.contract: AI provider registry, roles, and tool schema contract
entries.summary: The AI package exposes stable chat roles, a provider registry keyed by provider name, and tool metadata/schema structures consumed by agents and tool-aware models.

entries.contract: AI error sentinels and structured error values
entries.summary: The AI package exposes eight sentinel errors plus ToolError and ModelError structured values for provider and tool failures.

entries.contract: resource/action naming and request wire contract
entries.summary: Resource names, RPC/REST action strings, request versioning, params/meta decoding, REST X-Meta headers, and Bearer token locations are public API contracts for all framework resources.

entries.contract: approval admin DTO JSON wire shape
entries.summary: The approval/admin package defines the response DTOs used by approval/admin actions, including instance, task, detail, action-log, and metrics JSON field names.

entries.contract: approval my-task DTO JSON wire shape
entries.summary: The approval/my package defines the current-user self-service response DTOs used by approval/my actions such as find_pending_tasks, find_completed_tasks, and get_instance_detail.

entries.contract: approval module built-in resource/action surface
entries.summary: ApprovalModule registers approval/category, approval/delegation, approval/flow, approval/instance, approval/my, and approval/admin RPC resources with documented action names, permissions, tenancy constraints, and rate limits.

entries.contract: approval.* event topics and event payload shape
entries.summary: Approval domain events use approval.* topics, publish through event.WithTx, and expose JSON payload fields such as tenantId and occurredTime for downstream subscribers.

entries.contract: approval tenant and business-binding integration contract
entries.summary: Approval authorization distinguishes approval:super_admin from tenant-scoped users; business-bound flows use BusinessRefProvider for opaque refs, BusinessRefResolver for write-back record ids, and engine-owned status write-back.

entries.contract: approval 40xxx result-envelope and plain sentinel surface
entries.summary: Approval exposes four importable plain Go sentinels from the approval package, while built-in approval resources return internal 40xxx result.Error codes and i18n message keys through the API envelope.

entries.contract: approval flow/form JSON graph contract
entries.summary: FlowDefinition and form definitions are JSON wire shapes for visual workflow editors, including React Flow-style nodes/edges, node kind data, binding modes, and form field definitions.

entries.contract: cache TTL, eviction, invalidation, and singleflight behavior
entries.summary: Cache constructors expose memory and Redis implementations with TTL, eviction, serializer, invalidation, Close, and GetOrLoad/singleflight behavior documented as runtime contracts.

entries.contract: vef-cli root command, registered subcommands, and version output
entries.summary: The CLI root command is vef-cli, registers create, generate-build-info, and generate-model-schema, and exposes --version output with banner, Version, optional Built metadata, and -dirty suffix behavior.

entries.contract: generate-build-info flags, Git fallbacks, and generated Go source shape
entries.summary: generate-build-info exposes --output/-o and --package/-p defaults, creates missing output directories, reads Git version/commit with dev and none fallbacks, and emits BuildInfo = &monitor.BuildInfo{AppVersion, BuildTime, GitCommit}.

entries.contract: create command placeholder flags and not-implemented behavior
entries.summary: create is registered as a CLI command but is not implemented; it exposes --name/-n as required, --path/-p defaulting to ., --module/-m defaulting to empty, and returns the documented not-implemented error.
terms: vef-cli create is not implemented yet, please generate the project manually

entries.contract: generate-model-schema flags, model tag grammar, and generated schema helper API
entries.summary: generate-model-schema exposes required --input/--output flags, --package default schemas, file/direct directory modes, non-recursive *.go directory scanning, orm.BaseModel selection, bun table/alias/column/embed/rel/scanonly rules, label comments, exported schema variables, unexported schema types, alias/raw field accessors, Table/Alias/As/Columns helpers, Col collision prefixes, and __ keyword prefixes.

entries.contract: configuration keys, environment override, and effective defaults
entries.summary: The config package exposes stable vef.* keys, config tags, environment variables, supported database/storage/provider values, and Effective* methods/defaults used at boot.

entries.contract: request-scoped context values and Fiber/std context behavior
entries.summary: contextx stores and reads request-scoped values through Fiber Locals or context.WithValue, with documented defaults for request ID, principal, logger, DB, data-permission applier, method, path, and IP.

entries.contract: copy options, default converters, and custom converter append behavior
entries.summary: copier.Copy starts with case-sensitive matching and built-in value-to-pointer and pointer-to-value converters for common scalar, decimal, time, and timex types; options control ignore-empty, deep copy, case-insensitive matching, field-name mapping, and appended custom converters.

entries.contract: column alias string formatting and duplicate/foreign-key detection
entries.summary: dbx.ColumnWithAlias returns alias.column when alias is present and the raw column otherwise; duplicate and foreign-key helpers detect PostgreSQL and MySQL driver codes plus fallback message patterns for SQLite, SQL Server, and Oracle.

entries.contract: NewFromAny supported input families and unsupported-type behavior
entries.summary: decimal.Decimal is a shopspring/decimal v1.4.0 type alias with re-exported constructors, aggregators, constants, and inherited methods; NewFromAny accepts common Go input families, treats nil *Decimal as Zero, parses textual inputs, and reports unsupported values with decimal: unsupported type while MustFromAny panics.

entries.contract: hash and HMAC algorithm choices with hex-encoded output
entries.summary: hashx exposes MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-512, and SM3 hash helpers plus matching HMAC helpers, with string and byte input variants returning hex-encoded strings.

entries.contract: embedded locale file set
entries.summary: locales.EmbedLocales embeds the framework-shipped JSON locale catalogs used by i18n.New and the default translator, including the built-in zh-CN and en catalogs.

entries.contract: ID generator output formats, defaults, and Snowflake environment behavior
entries.summary: id.Generate uses XID by default, GenerateUUID returns UUID v7, random IDs default to a 62-character alphabet and length 32, and Snowflake uses a Base36 string with epoch/node/step settings and VEF_NODE_ID node selection.

entries.contract: logger interfaces and log level string values
entries.summary: logx defines the Logger and LoggerConfigurable interfaces expected by framework integrations and maps Level values to debug/info/warn/error/panic strings, with unknown for unrecognized values.

entries.contract: inclusive range state and relationship behavior
entries.summary: monad.Range is inclusive at both ends, treats start <= end as valid/non-empty, checks containment and overlap with inclusive comparisons, and returns the max/min intersection only when ranges overlap.

entries.contract: pointer helper nil, fallback, and equality behavior
entries.summary: ptr.Of returns nil for zero values, Value walks fallback pointers before returning zero, ValueOrElse calls a lazy fallback only for nil, Equal compares pointed values, and Coalesce returns the first non-nil pointer.

entries.contract: adapter callbacks, root/orphan handling, cycle protection, and search behavior
entries.summary: tree.Build uses Adapter callbacks to index nodes, treats nil or missing parent IDs as roots, skips empty IDs for indexing, protects recursive child assignment with visited tracking, and FindNode/FindNodePath search by adapter children.

entries.contract: framework version constant value
entries.summary: version.VEFVersion exposes the framework version string used by consumers that need to report or compare framework version.

entries.contract: CQRS typed action registry and behavior ordering
entries.summary: CQRS actions are registered by concrete command/query type, sent through behavior middleware, and report handler-not-found or result-type mismatch through public sentinel errors.

entries.contract: cron scheduler job definition and option contract
entries.summary: Cron job definitions wrap gocron schedules with stable one-time, duration, random-duration, and cron-expression constructors plus name/task/tag/concurrency/start/stop options.

entries.contract: CRUD builder action strings, REST override grammar, and import/export contracts
entries.summary: CRUD builders expose fixed RPC/REST action constants, REST override caveats, import/export params and format behavior, validation failure response shape, and hook execution points.

entries.contract: cryptox algorithm mode, key encoding, and error contract
entries.summary: cryptox constructors expose stable cipher/signer interfaces, algorithm-specific key encodings, AES/RSA modes, SM4 CBC interop IV behavior, ECIES curve identifiers, and error sentinels.

entries.contract: CSV importer/exporter option behavior
entries.summary: CSV import/export defaults to comma delimiters, header rows, framework-level trimming on import, comment handling disabled, header output, LF line endings on export, FieldsPerRecord=-1, and full-input ReadAll before parsing; WithSkipRows clamps negative values to zero, WithoutHeader uses positional mapping, NewMapImporter accepts mapOpts while NewMapExporter does not, and final writer flush errors are wrapped.

entries.contract: dynamic datasource provider and registry mutation contract
entries.summary: The datasource package reserves PrimaryName/primary, loads Provider specs during startup, exposes controlled dynamic registry mutation, supports non-mutating probes and parallel health checks, and defines reconcile/dry-run/close-grace behavior with explicit sentinel errors.

entries.contract: Inbox repository status/acquire/dedupe contract
entries.summary: The inbox package defines durable idempotency records keyed by event ID and consumer group, with processing/completed statuses, acquire results, lock ownership, and lock-loss errors.

entries.contract: event middleware chain ordering and trace context contract
entries.summary: Event middleware exposes publish/consume chain helpers, built-in order constants, recover/logging/tracing/metrics/inbox toggles, and context helpers for trace IDs.

entries.contract: memory transport name and queue-full policies
entries.summary: The memory transport registers as memory and exposes queue size, publish timeout, and full-policy options error, block, and drop_oldest.

entries.contract: outbox transport name, status lifecycle, and sink relay contract
entries.summary: The outbox transport registers as outbox, persists publish-only records in the caller transaction, tracks pending/processing/completed/failed/dead statuses, and relays to a sink transport.

entries.contract: Redis Streams transport name, stream prefix, group, and consumer config
entries.summary: The Redis Stream transport registers as redis_stream and exposes stream prefix, group/consumer, claim, start-id, max-len, and blocking read settings.

entries.contract: event transport frame, delivery, and capability contract
entries.summary: Event transports exchange Frame values, advertise delivery capabilities, and distinguish at-least-once, publish-only, group, and transactional behavior through Capabilities and TxTransport.

entries.contract: event type, envelope, routing, and publish/subscribe option contract
entries.summary: Event publishing validates EventTypePattern, builds Envelope metadata, applies routing and async/transactional options, and enforces SubscribeTyped/group semantics for durable transports.

entries.contract: Excel importer/exporter option and native cell behavior
entries.summary: Excel import reads by sheet name or index with name taking precedence, clamps negative skip rows, trims by default, supports positional mapping without headers, and reports ErrSheetIndexOutOfRange for invalid index; map import passes mapOpts into NewMapAdapterFromSpecs; export writes native typed cells only for default formatter columns without explicit Format, Formatter, or FormatterFn.

entries.contract: expression engine evaluation, value conversion, and mold transformer contract
entries.summary: The expression package exposes a pure-Go expr-lang-backed engine whose Compile validates eagerly, whose Value helpers decode JSON-like numbers, and whose expr mold transformer uses escaped comma parameters.

entries.contract: Fiber content-type helpers and trusted-proxy IP resolution
entries.summary: httpx exposes request helpers that resolve content type and client IP using Fiber behavior and vef.app.trusted_proxies.

entries.contract: i18n language selection, message lookup, and fallback contract
entries.summary: The i18n package selects language from VEF_I18N_LANGUAGE or runtime SetLanguage, supports zh-CN and en, and falls back to the message ID when no translation is found.

entries.contract: Goja JavaScript engine, module registry, JSON tag mapping, and thread-safety contract
entries.summary: The js package embeds Goja with preloaded libraries, CommonJS-style module support, JSON tag mapping for structs, value conversion helpers, and explicit non-thread-safe runtime guidance.

entries.contract: mapx JSON tag default, decode options, and built-in hooks
entries.summary: mapx converts structs and maps with json tags by default, supports tag/weak-typing/error options, and ships decode hooks for multipart files, raw JSON, decimal/time values, and collection sets.

entries.contract: MCP endpoint, provider groups, auth, built-in database query tool, built-in naming prompt, and schema contract
entries.summary: The MCP package exposes the /mcp Streamable HTTP endpoint, secure-by-default auth config, provider groups for tools/resources/prompts, ServerInfo defaults, SchemaFor/SchemaOf JSON schema generation, principal-aware DBWithOperator, the built-in database_query tool, and the built-in naming-master prompt.
terms: mcp: cannot generate schema for nil value

entries.contract: SPA middleware config and fallback static-serving contract
entries.summary: The middleware package exposes SPAConfig values supplied through vef:spa helpers for static roots, route prefixes, index fallback, and segment-boundary ExcludePaths prefixes.

entries.contract: dictionary cache invalidation event
entries.summary: CachedDictionaryResolver wraps a DictionaryLoader and invalidates cached dictionaries through DictionaryChangedEvent; empty Keys clears all cached dictionaries.

entries.contract: Transformer.Struct and Transformer.Field input/traversal behavior
entries.summary: Struct requires a non-nil pointer to a non-time struct; Field requires a non-nil pointer unless the tag string is empty or '-'. Struct traversal recurses into nested structs automatically, but slice/map element recursion requires dive.

entries.contract: mold struct tag grammar
entries.summary: The mold tag uses comma-separated transformer tags, function=param parameters, '-' ignore tags, 'dive' collection recursion, and 'keys'/'endkeys' map-key recursion; commas inside params are escaped as 0x2C.

entries.contract: translate transformer kind and target field convention
entries.summary: The built-in translate field transformer writes to a sibling <Field>Name field; the built-in dictionary translator supports only dict: kinds, and '?' only skips when no translator supports the full kind string.

entries.contract: sys/monitor built-in resource, action names, rate limits, DTO fields, and error codes
entries.summary: The monitor module registers sys/monitor actions with max 60 rate limits and exposes JSON DTO shapes plus ErrNotReady and ErrCollectionFailed API errors.

entries.contract: ORM query builder, model tag, operator, and transaction contract
entries.summary: The orm package re-exports the framework query builders, model helpers, operator placeholders, bun tag model conventions, and RunInTx/transaction behavior used by CRUD and application services.

entries.contract: pagination request and response JSON shape
entries.summary: The page package defines Pageable request metadata, Page[T] response shape, normalization, offset, and items/total JSON fields.

entries.contract: password encoder ID prefix, composite dispatch, and upgrade contract
entries.summary: Password encoders use the {algorithm}encoded_value family of formats such as {bcrypt}..., dispatch through EncoderID, support composite verification/upgrade, and expose bcrypt cost limits.

entries.contract: cast alias zero-value and error contract
entries.summary: reflectx exposes spf13/cast conversion aliases where E-suffixed helpers return errors and non-E helpers return zero values on conversion failure.

entries.contract: decimal conversion nil and error contract
entries.summary: reflectx decimal conversion maps nil and nil pointer/interface inputs to decimal.Zero, delegates non-nil values to decimal.NewFromAny, and ToDecimal discards conversion errors.

entries.contract: type compatibility and conversion contract
entries.summary: reflectx type helpers define one-level type indirection, pointer-to-struct checks, generic base-name similarity, pointer/value compatibility, and ErrCannotConvertType wrapping on unsupported conversions.

entries.contract: method discovery contract
entries.summary: reflectx method helpers find methods on values or addressable pointer copies and collect promoted pointer/value methods from struct pointer method sets.

entries.contract: string field accessor contract
entries.summary: reflectx string helpers identify string, string slice, and string map fields, return false for incompatible or nil values, no-op on incompatible setters, and replace *string pointers with fresh pointers.

entries.contract: empty, equality, and containment contract
entries.summary: reflectx value helpers define emptiness across nil/zero/collection/struct values, numeric equality within categories only, and containment for strings, slices, arrays, and maps with convertible keys.

entries.contract: reflection visitor traversal-mode, dive-tag, and depth contract
entries.summary: reflectx visitors expose depth-first and breadth-first traversal modes, traversal actions, dive tag selection, recursive disabling, maximum depth, absolute field index paths, cycle protection, and typed struct/field/method visitor callbacks.

entries.contract: result envelope JSON, error code, status, and option contract
entries.summary: The result package serializes API responses as code/message/data, uses stable default codes, exposes status/options behavior, and compares result.Error values by code.

entries.contract: sys/schema RPC resource
entries.summary: The schema module registers sys/schema with list_tables, get_table_schema, and list_views actions; each action has a custom Max 60 rate limit and uses the standard RPC result envelope.

entries.contract: schema DTO JSON wire shape
entries.summary: Public schema DTO JSON tags define the response contract, including omitempty behavior for optional schema, comment, constraint, default, action, and view columns fields.

entries.contract: primary data source schema inspection
entries.summary: Default schema inspection uses the primary data source only and supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQLite with dialect-specific schema and view inspection behavior.

entries.contract: search struct tag grammar and query operator contract
entries.summary: The search package parses search tags with operator, column, params, delimiter/type options, dive recursion, ignore markers, and documented operator names such as notBetween and iContains.

entries.contract: authentication resource, Bearer token locations, JWT token/claim shape, signature request contract, challenge flow, and security error envelope
entries.summary: Documents the built-in security/auth RPC actions, auth request/response fields, token types, JWT compact claims, access/refresh subject format, principal JSON behavior, challenge token stores, signature headers/payload/defaults, IP whitelist fail modes, and security-domain error codes/statuses.

entries.contract: RBAC permission loading, role-permission cache invalidation, user info/menu DTOs, and login audit events
entries.summary: Documents RolePermissionsLoader and CachedRolePermissionsLoader behavior, role invalidation event topic and empty-roles meaning, RBAC nil/role checks, data-scope priority winner, get_user_info DTO shapes, enum values, and login event topic/payload.

entries.contract: data permission scope keys, priorities, resolver winner, and request-scoped applier skip/error paths
entries.summary: Documents DataScope/DataPermissionResolver/DataPermissionApplier public contracts, built-in all/self scope keys, exact priorities, default created_by column, supports behavior, and RequestScopedDataPermApplier skip/error semantics.

entries.contract: sequence rule, reset, overflow, memory store, and error contract
entries.summary: The sequence package defines rule fields, reset cycles, overflow strategies, Generate/GenerateN behavior, the in-memory store, registration, and rule/overflow/count errors.

entries.contract: sort order text/JSON parsing and order spec contract
entries.summary: sortx defines asc/desc direction constants, null-ordering values, text/JSON marshaling, and OrderSpec fields used by search and CRUD sorting.

entries.contract: sys/storage upload actions, proxy URLs, meta tags, ACL, claim/delete lifecycle, and events
entries.summary: The storage module exposes sys/storage multipart upload actions, /storage/files/<key> proxy semantics, pub/priv key visibility, meta-tagged file reference extraction, transactional claim/delete lifecycle, and storage events.

entries.contract: compact key/value and struct-tag parser grammar
entries.summary: strx parses comma-separated key/value tag strings, supports custom pair/value delimiters, default keys, bare-as-key/value behavior, and space-delimited pairs.

entries.contract: dynamic ColumnSpec schema validation and map-row validation
entries.summary: NewSchemaFromSpecs requires unique non-empty Key values, non-nil Type values, and unique resolved names; map import validates Required, Validators, and optional RowValidator errors with errors.Join.

entries.contract: header mapping and row import behavior
entries.summary: BuildHeaderMapping trims headers when configured, skips empty and unknown headers, rejects duplicate non-empty headers, and WithoutHeader uses DefaultPositionalMapping; ParseRow reports deterministic row-level ImportError values without committing partial rows.

entries.contract: tabular struct tag grammar
entries.summary: The tabular tag uses comma-separated key=value pairs with a bare default column name, '-' to ignore a field, and 'dive' to flatten nested struct fields; semicolons are not separators.

entries.contract: Date, Time, and DateTime JSON/text format and comparison contract
entries.summary: timex wraps civil date/time values with strict JSON/text formats based on time.DateOnly, time.TimeOnly, and time.DateTime, plus open-interval Between and civil comparisons.

entries.contract: built-in validation tags, i18n labels, and custom rule registration
entries.summary: The validator package registers framework-specific validation tags for phone, decimal, alphanumeric, label, and i18n label rules, plus public APIs for custom validation rules.

entries.contract: top-level DI extension group names
entries.summary: Top-level Provide/Supply helpers contribute to stable FX groups for API resources, middleware, event transports, MCP providers, and approval lifecycle hooks.

entries.contract: vef.Run boot graph and lifecycle timeouts
entries.summary: vef.Run appends user options to the framework boot graph, uses default 30s start and 60s stop timeouts, and keeps ApprovalModule opt-in rather than part of the default graph.
